calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion
Off Calendar Mar-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant SEREC of California, a California corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768.

William T. Webb, Esq. appeared for plaintiff GCL Solar Energy, Inc., a California corporation.
Ann Adrineda, Financial Controller, and Han Yang Xu, Senior Project Compliance Analyst, witnesses for plaintiff, were placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defaulted defendant SEREC of California, a California corporation.
Plaintiff's complaint was filed on January 12, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant SEREC of California, a California corporation, on March 1, 2017.
Plaintiff's first cause of action for Preliminary Injunction was granted on February 8, 2017.
DOE defendants and plaintiff's third cause of action for Breach of Contract were dismissed on January 8, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff GCL Solar Energy, Inc., a California corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendant SEREC of California, a California corporation, as follows:

Defendant SEREC of California, a California corporation, is enjoined and restrained from asserting, as to plaintiff GCL Solar Energy, Inc., any claimed lien rights pursuant to California Commericial Code Section 7206, and specifically from selling any such property pursuant to Commercial Code Section 7210, pending further Order from this Court. If defendant SEREC of California believes it has good cause to exercise any such rights, it must first apply to this Court for relief from this injunction.

It is further Ordered that defendant SEREC of California shall be subject to the provisions of this Permanent Injunction when it has been personnaly served with a copy of this Permanent Injunction.

Proposed Court Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Continued To Mar-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion And Motion
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Continued To Mar-13-18 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 514

Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jan-09-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-25-2017 Continued To Oct-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Osc Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. OSC Regarding Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
